body_txt: Substitute teachers in fiction have it bad. On top of having to deal with a class and material you are unfamiliar with, they also seem to be the butt of their jokes and pranks. Kids feel free to goof around, ignore, annoy, or even talk back to substitutes in ways they never would their main teachers. After all, it's unlikely the students'll ever see them again, so they feel just fine acting laid back and disrespectful around them. The substitute may be a recurring character however, especially in the Two-Teacher School where casting space is often too valuable for a purely throwaway character note The latter case is Truth in Television often, as due to the job often being a thankless task as shown by this trope existing, and often not even getting a consistent paycheck for their troubles, the pool of substitute teachers at any given moment for a school is often slim. In some smaller districts, there may even only be the singular substitute available, leaving plenty of chances to gather a rapport with students. If a substitute isn't a drill sergeant-tier disciplinarian or Sadist Teacher then they'll often succumb to this, and even then, they may not be immune. Indeed, this treatment may be the reason they became so harsh in the first place, or their infamous reputation may encourage students to challenge their authority as a dare from their classmates. Truth in Television , obviously. Compare Dreaded Kids' Party Entertainer Job . This has nothing to do with that kind of teasing.
